channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel intercepts Houthi missile fired from Yemen
item.description: Israel’s military said it intercepted a missile fired toward the country by Yemen’s Houthi rebels, setting off sirens late Monday in central areas including Tel Aviv.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that released hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: World population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israeli spy network uncovers Hezbollah commander's plans to marry his 4 mistresses
item.description: Israeli spy agencies infiltrated Hezbollah so successfully that they learned intimate details of commander Fuad Shukr's plans to marry his four mistresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday its final weapons and aid package to Ukraine before President-elect Trump is set to take the reins in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Politician among protesters arrested in Kenya while calling for an end to alleged abductions
item.description: An opposition politician and several other protesters have been arrested in Kenya during street demonstrations calling for an end to alleged abductions, that have recently targeted young government critics.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lessons from the elections held in 70 countries in 2024
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When voters around the globe had their say in 2024, their message was often: “You’re fired.” Some 70 countries that are home to half the world’s population held elections this year, and in many incumbents were punished.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean authorities request arrest warrant for impeached President Yoon over martial law decree
item.description: Law enforcement authorities in South Korea requested an arrest warrant for President Yoon Suk Yeol after being impeached two weeks ago for his martial law decree on Dec. 3 that shook the nation. Authorities are investigating whether his actions that day amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia's incumbent president gains most votes for re-election, but not enough to avoid a runoff
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s incumbent President Zoran Milanovic won most of the votes in the first round of a presidential election on Sunday, but must face a runoff against a ruling party candidate to secure another five-year term.
item.dc:creator: DARKO BANDIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu completes prostate surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: The prostate surgery that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent on Sunday has been completed, Fox News has learned. The Israeli leader suffered a UTI before the procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden 'deeply saddened' by Jeju Air accident in South Korea
item.description: President Biden said he was “deeply saddened” by the Jeju Air accident in South Korea that left nearly 180 people dead. “Jill and I are deeply saddened to learn of the loss of life that occurred as a result of the Jeju Airlines accident in Muan, Republic of Korea,” Biden said Sunday in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Syria's de facto leader says it could take up to 4 years to hold elections
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syria's de facto leader said Sunday it could take up to four years to hold elections in Syria, and that he plans to dissolve his Islamist group that led the country's insurgency at an anticipated national dialogue summit for the country.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows the toughest anti-US policy before Trump takes office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to implement the “toughest” anti-U.S. policy, state media reported Sunday, less than a month before Donald Trump takes office as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the deadly passenger jet crash in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A passenger jet burst into flames while landing at an airport in South Korea on Sunday, killing 179 people in one of the deadliest air disasters in that nation's history. There were only two survivors, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim Jong Un vows to launch 'toughest' anti-US policy
item.description: Kim Jong Un vowed to launch a tough anti-U.S. strategy in the coming years, as President-elect Trump prepares to enter his second term in office. During a five-day Workers’ Party meeting this past week, Kim said North Korea would launch “the toughest anti-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: North Korea vows 'toughest' US policy in vague announcement
item.description: North Korean Dictator Kim Jong Un's regime vowed to adopt its "toughest" policies against the U.S. during its annual meeting last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu to undergo major surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will undergo prostate removal surgery on Sunday amid multiple conflicts across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kazakhstan plane crash survivors say they heard bangs before aircraft went down, Putin issues statement
item.description: Survivors of the Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed in Kazakhstan on Christmas Day say they heard at least one loud bang before the aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation
